We spent a good 2-3 hours strolling through the 4 levels looking at all the movie memorabilia. Displays included lots of information, props, costumes, and awards for all sorts of genres. Both historic and modern. There's also a section on make up, which given it's in the old Max Factor factory is not surprising. Each level is themed. You travel at your own pace. The morning we went wasn't very busy so no problems seeing the exhibits or taking your time etc. Extraordinary value for money compared to other attractions we visited. Well worth visiting as not only interesting but also a good trip down memory lane for some of the more historic stuff.

4 stories of memorabilia in the old Max Factor bldg. A bit shabby, but a must-see for classic film fans - specifically the main floor (chockfull of old Hollywood artifacts/clothes/photos). The horror display in the basement is 2nd rate, 3rd floor has more classics, 4th is comedy.

This was incredibly enjoyable and really lets you feel the vibe of vintage Hollywood. Whether you’re familiar with actresses like Marilyn Monroe, Lucille Ball, and Lana Turner or not, seeing their personal possessions, costume jewellery, outfits, and old publicity photos gives you a real insight into old Hollywood.

There are also plenty of props from classic films like Rocky, The Godfather, and Casablanca, which makes it even more interesting if you’re into film history. Overall, it’s a must for anyone interested in Hollywood, and it’s great that it’s independently run as well.
